title: Dozens of Polymarket Bets Show Signs of Insider Trading, The Times Finds
description: Dozens of long-shot bets on Polymarket, from the war with Iran to the cryptocurrency market, have defied the odds, according to a New York Times examination.
author: Stuart A. Thompson and David Yaffe-Bellany

title: Meta Reassigns 7,000 Employees to Focus on A.I.
description: The company announced the changes two days before it plans to lay off 10 percent of its work force, or about 8,000 employees.
author: Eli Tan

title: Five Takeaways From the Blockbuster Trial Pitting Elon Musk Against OpenAI
description: It took a jury less than two hours to decide that Mr. Musk had waited too long to sue. But the testimony over three weeks was still illuminating.
author: Cade Metz

title: After Elon Musk’s Court Loss Comes the Long Hot A.I. Summer
description: Even as protests increase, the collapse of Mr. Musk’s suit against OpenAI and Sam Altman will speed up the artificial intelligence juggernaut.
author: David Streitfeld and Natallie Rocha

title: Elon Musk Loses $150 Billion Suit Against OpenAI and Sam Altman
description: A nine-member jury found that Mr. Musk had waited too long to sue. The setback for the tech mogul frees OpenAI to continue in the artificial intelligence race.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

title: What to Know About Elon Musk’s Trial Against OpenAI
description: A jury in Oakland, Calif., reached a decision after a three-week-long trial seen as pivotal for the future of OpenAI and the artificial intelligence race.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac

title: Here’s what was on the jury’s list for a decision.
author: Natallie Rocha

title: Peter G. Neumann, Who Warned of Computer Security Risks, Dies at 93
description: For decades, he criticized the industry’s lax attitudes toward computer security and individual digital privacy. He also developed solutions.
author: John Markoff

title: What the jury had to weigh to decide the case.
author: Cade Metz

title: Peter G. Neumann, Who Warned of Computer Security Risks, Dies at 93
description: For decades, he criticized the industry’s lax attitudes toward both computer security and individual digital privacy. And he developed solutions.
author: John Markoff

title: Did You Mean to Share That Selfie? What to Know About Instagram Instants.
description: “Instants” blasts photos to your mutual followers or close friends, immediately. Here’s how to avoid accidental embarrassment.
author: Hannah Ziegler

title: OpenAI Bought Company That Offered A.I. Tools for Cloning Voices
description: The acquisition, Weights.gg, was a sort of social network for creating and sharing artificial intelligence algorithms.
author: Mike Isaac

title: A Start-Up Aiming to Make Geothermal Energy Mainstream Goes Public
description: Fervo Energy, which uses drilling techniques from the oil and gas industry to produce power from the earth’s heat, raised $1.9 billion in an initial public offering.
author: Ivan Penn and Brad Plumer

title: A.I. and Humans Battle It Out in a Cybersecurity Showdown
description: Experts and college students used A.I. agents to try to break into and defend computer networks in a national competition. The agents did all right on their own, too.
author: Cade Metz and Mikayla Whitmore

title: Why ‘Smart’ Products Have Started to Look Like the Dumb Choice
description: How Wi-Fi-connecting, app-based tech led to a backlash in the name of simplicity.
author: Nitsuh Abebe

title: Nvidia’s Future in China Remains Unclear After Trump-Xi Summit
description: The standoff comes as Chinese firms increasingly turn to domestic chipmakers like Huawei, in a drive to reduce China’s dependence on Western technologies.
author: Meaghan Tobin

title: A.I. Safety Is So Back + Mythos Mayhem with Nikesh Arora + Hot Mess Express
description: After several years of dismissing A.I. safety as doomer fear-mongering, parts of the Trump administration now seem ready to support regulation.
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Vjeran Pavic, Chris Wood, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop and Rowan Niemisto

title: U.S. and China Will Start Discussing A.I. Safety, Bessent Says
description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent did not say when these talks would happen. There are fears in the United States and China about the threats from A.I., but neither side is willing to slow down its development.
author: Vivian Wang

title: OpenAI Trial Heads to Jury After Closing Arguments in Musk vs. Altman Case
description: Lawyers for Elon Musk and OpenAI made their closing arguments in the blockbuster federal trial on Thursday. Nine jurors are set to begin deliberations next week.
author: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
